results.title: The Dead bird
results.creator_credit: Graham Parker, Moreland-Latchford Productions and Oxford Films (U.S.)
results.brief_description: A short story by Margaret Wise Brown which encourages young readers to expect a rewarding experience from reading and to write a story themselves from casual incidents in their own lives. The theme of the film, the dead bird, also lends itself to discussion about the naturalness of death, leading to an appraisal of the essential values in living

results.title: De Renoir a Picasso
results.creator_credit: Paul Haesaerts and Jean Van Raemdonck
results.brief_description: Part of the “Art et cinema” series, this film presents a study and comparison of the characteristics of Renoir the sensualist, Seurat, the intellectual, and Picasso, the instructive artist, the three main inspirational sources for modern French art. The soft, rounded forms of Renoir are related to the Greek ideal of physical beauty, Seurat’s severe simplification are shown to echo Byzantine style; and Picasso’s distortions are traced back to similar experiments in primitive art.
results.description: Part of the “Art et cinema” series, this film presents a study and comparison of the characteristics of Renoir the sensualist, Seurat, the intellectual, and Picasso, the instructive artist, the three main inspirational sources for modern French art. results.title: Intransit
results.creator_credit: Mike Daly, Isabel Perez and AFTRS
results.brief_description:
A boy journeys into his own dark underworld where he is confronted by the vengeful father of his imagination.
Features Louis Westgarth as Ben, James Hagen as Ben’s father and Humphrey Westgarth as young Ben.

results.title: Binary
results.creator_credit: Mike Daly, Rachel Roberts and AFTRS
results.brief_description:
Trapped in a cell of fear, Claire’s longing for connection propels her beyond the known world.
The world of Binary is neither a particular time or place, it is Claire’s headspace. The images, sounds and content of the film always represent Claire’s mental state, they are completely subjective. Digital visual effects were employed in the production of Binary to assist this approach and to portray Claire’s increasingly unstable view of her world.
Features Susan Prior as Claire and Briden Aspinall as the young girl.
